blocks.content:
Cassandra sat on the edge of her bed, one leg tucked beneath her, Harold resting loosely against her side.

Her phone lit up again.
Cassandra stared at the message for a second before unlocking her screen.

messages.content: He asked if he’d see me again.

blocks.content:
Three dots appeared immediately.

Stayed longer this time.

Then:

Cassandra’s thumb hovered over the screen.

She knew that tone.

Even in text.

messages.content: I’m not trying to ruin anything.

blocks.content:
Cassandra looked down at Harold, adjusting him slightly in her arms.

Soft.

Steady.

Simple.

“I don’t need protection,” she muttered under her breath.

Then typed:

messages.content: I don’t want to be protected from everything.

blocks.content:
The reply took longer this time.

When it came—

it was quieter.

Cassandra’s chest tightened slightly.

Not enough to stop her.

Enough to notice.

She leaned back slowly against the headboard, eyes drifting toward the ceiling.

After.

She didn’t like that word.

It implied something finished.

Something final.

Something she wasn’t ready to name.

Her fingers moved again.
